content: schwab-py v1.5.1 (released June 30, 2025), Python >=3.10, actively maintained (MIT, authored by Alex Golec). PyPI classifier says '1-Planning' but that is stale metadata -- library ships 38 releases and has Discord community support. REST surface: get_account()/get_accounts() with fields=Account.Fields.POSITIONS for positions, get_transactions()/get_transaction(), get_option_chain(), get_price_history() + variants (every_minute/five/ten/fifteen/thirty/day/week), get_quote()/get_quotes(), get_market_hours(), get_instruments()/get_instrument_by_cusip(), get_orders_for_account(). Auth: client_from_login_flow (browser OAuth, not headless), client_from_token_file (existing token file, auto-refresh), client_from_manual_flow (headless-compatible: manual URL copy-paste), client_from_access_functions (custom token_read_func/token_write_func for serverless/Postgres storage). Access token auto-refresh is transparent -- schwab-py detects expiry before API calls and refreshes invisibly. 7-day refresh token expiry raises OAuthError: invalid_client: refresh token invalid -- must delete token and re-auth. Headless re-auth: run client_from_manual_flow() on a browser machine, copy token to server OR use client_from_access_functions with Postgres as token store. Token format is JSON; docs say to serialize/deserialize without inspecting internals.
